ddls:
CREATE TABLE "violations" (
  "violation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each violation */,
  "boroid" INTEGER /* Identifier for the borough */,
  "boro" TEXT /* Name of the borough */,
  "housenumber" INTEGER /* House number of the property */,
  "streetname" TEXT /* Street name of the property */,
  "zip" INTEGER /* ZIP code of the property */,
  "apartment" TEXT /* Apartment number (if applicable) */,
  "block" INTEGER /* Block number of the property */,
  "lot" INTEGER /* Lot number of the property */,
  "class" TEXT /* Class of the violation */,
  "novtype" TEXT /* Type of the Notice of Violation (NOV) */,
  "violationstatus" TEXT /* Status of the violation (e.g., Open, Close) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the property */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the property */,
  "communityboard" INTEGER /* Community board number */,
  "councildistrict" INTEGER /* Council district number */,
  "censustract" INTEGER /* Census tract number */,
  "bin" TEXT /* Building identification number (BIN) */,
  "bbl" TEXT /* Borough-block-lot (BBL) number */,
  "nta" TEXT /* Neighborhood tabulation area (NTA) */,
  "violation_date" TEXT /* Date when the violation was issued */,
  "inspection_date" TEXT /* Date when the last inspection was conducted */,
  "property_owner_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the property_owners table */,
  "inspector_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the inspectors table */,
  "fine_amount" TEXT /* Amount of the fine associated with the violation */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for resolving the violation */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the violation was resolved */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the violation */,
  "evidence" TEXT /* Any evidence or documents related to the violation */,
  "priority_level" TEXT /* Priority level of the violation (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("violation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_violations_property_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("property_owner_id") REFERENCES property_owners ("owner_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_violations_inspector_id FOREIGN KEY ("inspector_id") REFERENCES inspectors ("inspector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_violations_violationstatus FOREIGN KEY ("violationstatus") REFERENCES violation_statuses ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_violations_class FOREIGN KEY ("class") REFERENCES violation_classes ("class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_violations_boroid FOREIGN KEY ("boroid") REFERENCES boroughs ("boroid"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_violations_novtype FOREIGN KEY ("novtype") REFERENCES violation_types ("novtype_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "boroughs" (
  "boroid" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each borough */,
  "boro_name" TEXT /* Name of the borough */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the borough */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the borough */,
  "area" TEXT /* Area of the borough in square miles */,
  "num_properties" INTEGER /* Number of properties in the borough */,
  "num_violations" INTEGER /* Number of current violations in the borough */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("boroid")
)

CREATE TABLE "violation_classes" (
  "class_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the violation class */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the violation class */,
  "max_fine" TEXT /* Maximum fine amount for the violation class */,
  "min_fine" TEXT /* Minimum fine amount for the violation class */,
  "default_due_days" INTEGER /* Default number of days for resolving the violation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "violation_types" (
  "novtype_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each NOV type */,
  "novtype_name" TEXT /* Name of the NOV type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the NOV type */,
  "penalty" TEXT /* Standard penalty for the violation type */,
  "resolution_period" INTEGER /* Standard number of days for resolving the violation type */,
  "legal_reference" TEXT /* Legal reference or statute for the violation type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("novtype_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "violation_statuses" (
  "status_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the violation status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the violation status */,
  "next_steps" TEXT /* Next steps required for the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., inspector, property owner, legal staff) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object or text field outlining user permissions */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "violation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the violation being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the violation */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the violation was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, update) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the device used for access */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_violation_id FOREIGN KEY ("violation_id") REFERENCES violations ("violation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "violation_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history event */,
  "violation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the violation being tracked */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "old_status" TEXT /* Previous status of the violation */,
  "new_status" TEXT /* New status of the violation */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes about the change */,
  "old_fine_amount" TEXT /* Previous fine amount */,
  "new_fine_amount" TEXT /* New fine amount */,
  "old_due_date" TEXT /* Previous due date */,
  "new_due_date" TEXT /* New due date */,
  "old_priority_level" TEXT /* Previous priority level */,
  "new_priority_level" TEXT /* New priority level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_violation_history_violation_id FOREIGN KEY ("violation_id") REFERENCES violations ("violation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_violation_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "property_owners" (
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each property owner */,
  "owner_name" TEXT /* Name of the property owner */,
  "owner_email" TEXT /* Email address of the property owner */,
  "owner_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the property owner */,
  "owner_address" TEXT /* Address of the property owner */,
  "num_properties" INTEGER /* Number of properties owned by the owner */,
  "num_violations" INTEGER /* Number of current violations for the owner */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("owner_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inspectors" (
  "inspector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inspector */,
  "inspector_name" TEXT /* Name of the inspector */,
  "inspector_email" TEXT /* Email address of the inspector */,
  "inspector_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the inspector */,
  "inspector_address" TEXT /* Address of the inspector */,
  "num_inspections" INTEGER /* Number of inspections conducted by the inspector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inspector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "correspondence" (
  "correspondence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each correspondence */,
  "violation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the violation being tracked */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the correspondence */,
  "correspondence_date" TEXT /* Date of the correspondence */,
  "correspondence_type" TEXT /* Type of correspondence (e.g., email, letter, phone call) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the correspondence */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the correspondence (e.g., sent, received, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("correspondence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_correspondence_violation_id FOREIGN KEY ("violation_id") REFERENCES violations ("violation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_correspondence_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
